rulings.Case Title: BEATA KUREK et al VS. CHEVY CHASE BANK, F.S.B. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Demurrer And Demurrer To First Amended Complaint;
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, September 1, 2009, line 6, DEFENDANT CHEVY CHASE BANK, F.S.B. Demurrer To First Amended Complaint. Demurrer overruled in part and sustained in part. Demurrer overruled as to the first, second, fifth, seventh, and eighth causes of action. On demurrer, the factual allegations are accepted as true. The arguments of true beneficiary, the tender rule, and no duty sounding in negligence must be raised as affirmative defenses. Demurrer sustained with ten days leave to amend as to sixth cause of action. Plaintiffs must allege fraud with greater specificity. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: SERVICE EMPLOYEES INTERNATIONAL UNION, LOCAL 1000 et al VS. ARNOLD SCHWARZENEGGER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, September 1, 2009, line 8, RESPONDENT ARNOLD SCHWARZENEGGER, and, DAVID GILB DEMURRER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION. Demurrer overrruled. The doctrine of exclusive concurrent jurisdiction does not apply to this matter because the issue in this case was not adjudicated in the Sacramento case. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: SERVICE EMPLOYEES INTERNATIONAL UNION, LOCAL 1000 et al VS. ARNOLD SCHWARZENEGGER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion On Writ Of Mandate, Injunctive And Declaratory Relief By Service Employees International Union, Local 1000 Et Al, Request For Judicial Notice
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, September 1, 2009, line 8, PETITIONER SERVICE EMPLOYEES INTERNATIONAL UNION, LOCAL 1000 Motion on Writ of Mandate, Injunctive and Declaratory Relief by Service Employees International Union, Local 1000, et al. Motion granted. This court has the power to rule on the instant petitions, as Judge Busch's order was limited to adjudicating the rights of the CASE Union. This court has independently reviewed the record and authorities in this case and finds the doctrine of exclusive concurrent jurisdiction does not apply. The specific exemption under Insurance Code section 11873(c) for non-executive employees was not adjudicated in the Sacramento case. Insurance Code section 11873(c) exempts State Compensation Insurance Fund employees from the furlough. A "furlough" reduces the availability of staff and constitutes a "staff cutback." The petition is granted and the writ shall issue. =(302/CWW)
